reviewer_notes: Core FFmpeg DNN operand-index OOB mechanism is right, but parsed predicates and anchors only cover dnn_backend_native operand_index hunk in both commits and miss the layer loader operand index guards introduced via operands_num. Multi-fix set is structurally present but not semantically covered.

Status is rejected due function_binding: CodeAnchor names ReadXPMImage but function_id is missing. Patch hunk adds memset for target and symbolic in coders/xpm.c and semantic explanation is plausible, but gate rejects anchor, predicates, and fix_set.

curl UAF mechanism is correct: lastconnect pointer is replaced by lastconnect_id and Curl_getconnectinfo validates by cache lookup. Evidence link is incomplete because anchors omit multi.c add/remove/done/close_connect_only hunks that establish the stale connection lifecycle trigger.

httpd mod_proxy_uwsgi core root cause is supported by pktsize type widening and APR_UINT16_MAX guard in both fix commits. Unsupported caveat: model conflates CHANGES 16K wording with actual 65535 guard and overstates Buffer Overflow potential.

Parse failure is schema_validation_missing_path, not missing JSON: raw_response is fenced JSON, but code_anchors.1.path through code_anchors.4.path are absent. Raw semantics are plausible for KVM SEV reclaimed guest memory cache flush and stale cacheline risk, but schema rejection forces overall 0.

Linux bonding root cause is exact: bond_ipsec_add_sa dereferenced bond->curr_active_slave via slave->dev without a NULL check; patch adds if (!slave) unlock and return -ENODEV in drivers/net/bonding/bond_main.c.

openjpeg mechanism is supported: image, l_stream, l_codec, and cstr_index NULL initialization moved from main scope into the per-image loop. Direct evidence supports double-free on mixed valid/invalid image directories; UAF wording is unsupported overreach.

OpenSSL tls1_check_sig_alg NULL dereference is correctly supported by sigalg != NULL guard before sigalg->sigandhash. Minor unsupported/minimality issue: model generalizes NULL source to s->shared_sigalgs and omits TLS 1.3 SSL_check_chain callback trigger constraints.

QEMU ati_2d_blt direction is partly right, but the model treats all patch changes as root cause. bpp and src/dst stride zero checks are supported; division-by-zero, broad OOB, integer overflow/underflow, and coordinate update logic as CVE trigger are not directly supported.

Wireshark NFS recursion-depth mechanism is correct: NFS_MAX_FS_DEPTH and fs_depth proto_data guard stop excessive full-name recursion and set fs_cycle. Unsupported/minimality caveat: patch is depth-threshold based, not visited-set cycle detection; 8 anchors include include/expert registration plumbing.
